The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), signed into law on July 4, 2025, introduces new domestic content requirements for the Section 48E credit, which are harmonized with the Section 45Y credit. Specifically, the OBBBA mandates that facilities beginning construction after June 16, 2025, must meet certain domestic content thresholds to qualify for the 45Y Production Tax Credit.

The evidence indicates that the domestic content requirements for the Investment Tax Credits (ITC) will not become mandatory in 2025. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) passed in July 2025 includes changes to the tax credits but does not make the existing requirements mandatory. Instead, it phases out solar and wind tax credits over two years, with specific deadlines for projects to begin construction by the end of 2025 to receive the full credit.

The House of Representatives passed H.R. 1, the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' on May 22, 2025, which significantly amends the clean energy provisions of the Inflation Reduction Act. The bill accelerates the phase-out of the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X) and introduces FEOC restrictions that could limit the use of foreign materials or subcomponents.
